reforms.description: The government implements a business tax reform that broadens the tax base, reduces marginal tax rates, and abolishes the wealth tax on companies, alongside a series of measures to improve the profitability and capital base of small- and medium-sized companies, including special tax breaks on share purchases and reduced wealth tax on equity holdings.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance the business environment and improve profitability, which are expected to support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has implemented the business tax reform measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax reform represents a significant structural change in the business tax landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The Parliament decides to implement a business tax reform with implications for personal taxation with effect from 1 January 1992.
